career_angle: Don't let vendor blogs reshape your infra strategy. If you're evaluating distributed RL training, demand published benchmarks comparing distributed vs. single-cluster convergence times before buying the narrative.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Fireworks AI has real engineers but this is a corporate blog post, not peer-reviewed research or independent analysis.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: No benchmarks, no training runs, no convergence data, no comparisons. Pure theoretical hand-waving about 'hot-load updates' and 'compact deltas' with zero evidence any of it works at frontier scale.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Nothing to reproduce. No methodology, no architecture details, no code, no experimental setup. Just claims.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Fireworks sells distributed compute infrastructure. They are literally arguing that you don't need what they don't sell (mega clusters) and instead need what they do sell. Textbook conflict.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Meta, and xAI are all building mega clusters. Fireworks is arguing against the revealed preferences of every frontier lab with actual results.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Buzzword-heavy ('hot-load updates,' 'rollout fleets stay warm,' 'compact deltas') but no specific numbers, architectures, or implementation details.

dimensions.name: Unknown
dimensions.rationale: Fireworks is a legitimate inference company, but they have not produced a frontier model using this approach. The thesis is unproven by their own results.
